Config.MenuHint: One-stop window for every Gamma World system toggle: house rules, combat automation, roll visibility, and template lifecycle. Individual settings no longer appear in the default list — open this window to edit them.
Config.ResetConfirmBody: This resets every setting in this window to its out-of-the-box default. Your world data is not touched.
Config.ResetDone: Gamma World settings reset to defaults.

PilotAnimations.Name: Enable Pilot Animations
PilotAnimations.Hint: Play the system's Sequencer + JB2A combat and support effects for energy weapons, explosives, shields, and high-value Ancient gear when those modules are active.

PshTechReliable.Name: PSH technology always works once figured out
PshTechReliable.Hint: Homebrew: once a Pure Strain Human has analysed an Ancient artifact (operation known), further uses by that PSH actor bypass the condition and malfunction rolls. Non-PSH actors and unanalysed artifacts still follow RAW.

AutoRollNpcDamage.Name: Auto-roll damage for NPC attacks
AutoRollNpcDamage.Hint: When enabled, NPC (non-player-owned) attackers automatically roll damage right after a hit. Player characters always click their own damage button. HP never mutates until the GM clicks Apply Damage.

AutoTickFatigue.Name: Auto-tick fatigue per combat round
AutoTickFatigue.Hint: When enabled, each combatant's Fatigue round counter increments by 1 every new combat round. Disable to manage fatigue manually.

ResetFatigueOnCombatEnd.Name: Reset fatigue when combat ends
ResetFatigueOnCombatEnd.Hint: When enabled, every combatant's Fatigue round counter resets to 0 when the combat is deleted. Rest actions also clear fatigue regardless of this setting.

RestAdvancesWorldTime.Name: Rests advance world time
RestAdvancesWorldTime.Hint: When enabled (default), Short Rest advances world time by 1 hour and Long Rest by 6 hours. This causes hourly cell drain (Powered Plate, Energy Cloak, etc.) to tick during the rest. Disable for tables that prefer manual time control.

EncounterCloseSummary.Name: Encounter-close summary card
EncounterCloseSummary.Hint: When enabled (default), deleting a combat posts a GM-whisper chat card tallying XP from defeated monsters with a Distribute XP button + per-monster Roll Loot buttons. Disable for tables that prefer manual XP / loot tracking.

AutoPickDamageMultiplier.Name: Auto-pick damage multiplier from target traits
AutoPickDamageMultiplier.Hint: When enabled (default), the damage chat card pre-selects the right multiplier for each target based on the target's damage Resistance / Immunity / Vulnerability traits (×0 / ×½ / ×2; ×1 otherwise). Disable to force the GM to pick on every hit.

TravelLegHours.Name: Travel leg duration (hours)
TravelLegHours.Hint: Travel runs in legs of this length, with one wandering-encounter check per leg. RAW splits the day into 4-hour watches so 4 is the default. Lower for more frequent encounter checks; higher for sparser ones.

AutoApplyOnHitConditions.Name: Auto-apply on-hit save and condition effects
AutoApplyOnHitConditions.Hint: When enabled, hits from weapons with a special on-hit effect (poison, paralysis, radiation, stun, etc.) auto-post a save card: NPCs resolve locally, PCs get a save dialog. On fail the matching timed status effect is applied. Disable to keep the manual Apply Effect follow-up button.

GrenadePersistentRounds.Name: Default persistent grenade duration (rounds)
GrenadePersistentRounds.Hint: Number of rounds that gas or smoke grenade templates stay visible after detonation when the item itself does not specify a duration. Instantaneous grenades ignore this.

NpcDamageMode.Name: NPC damage auto-roll mode
NpcDamageMode.Hint: Controls when NPC (non-player-owned) attackers auto-roll damage after an attack card posts. None = never, On Hit = only on confirmed hits, Always = regardless of outcome (for save-based attacks without a to-hit roll). HP never mutates until the GM clicks Apply Damage.

PromptBeforeApplyDamage.Name: Confirm before applying damage to PCs
PromptBeforeApplyDamage.Hint: When enabled, clicking Apply Damage against a player-owned target pops a confirmation dialog with the amount. NPC auto-damage paths skip the prompt to avoid a modal per swing.

NpcSaveMode.Name: NPC save resolution mode
NpcSaveMode.Hint: Auto: NPC saves resolve immediately via the GM. Button: saves post a chat card with per-NPC Roll buttons the GM clicks manually (matches the AOE save card pattern).

PlayerSaveTimeout.Name: Player save dialog timeout (seconds)
PlayerSaveTimeout.Hint: Auto-resolve a stalled player save dialog after this many seconds. 0 = wait forever (default). Useful for play-by-post or when a player is AFK mid-combat.

AttackRollMode.Name: Default roll mode — attack rolls
AttackRollMode.Hint: Foundry chat visibility for attack rolls posted by the system. GM Roll hides from players; Blind hides from everyone including the GM's own chat log until revealed; Self Roll whispers only to the roller.

DamageRollMode.Name: Default roll mode — damage rolls
DamageRollMode.Hint: Foundry chat visibility for damage rolls posted by the system.

SaveRollMode.Name: Default roll mode — save rolls
SaveRollMode.Hint: Foundry chat visibility for save rolls posted by the system.

HideGmRollDetails.Name: Hide GM roll dice breakdowns
HideGmRollDetails.Hint: Strip the individual dice tooltips from GM-sourced roll messages so players see only the total. Use this to preserve mystery on monster rolls.

SuppressGmDiceAnimation.Name: Suppress GM dice animation
SuppressGmDiceAnimation.Hint: Hide the 3D dice animation (Dice So Nice or Foundry native) on GM-sourced rolls. Useful when pairing with GM Roll / Blind Roll visibility so players don't see the dice bounce for a hidden roll.

AutoRemoveInstantTemplate.Name: Auto-remove instantaneous AOE templates
AutoRemoveInstantTemplate.Hint: Delete a grenade or AOE template from the canvas immediately after its save card posts, as long as the item has no persistent-cloud duration. Disable if you want templates to linger until manually removed.

AutoConsumeCharges.Name: Auto-consume weapon ammo and artifact charges on use
AutoConsumeCharges.Hint: When enabled, firing a weapon with consumable ammo decrements its count and using an Ancient artifact with charges debits them. Disable to track resources manually (sandbox play, pretend-infinite arsenal).

SoundCuesEnabled.Name: Enable combat sound cues
SoundCuesEnabled.Hint: Master toggle for the sound-cue pipeline. When on, configured audio files play on the matching combat event (attack hit/miss/crit/fumble, damage applied, save success/fail, condition applied). Leave a specific cue's file empty to silence just that event.

SoundCueAttackHit.Name: Cue — attack hits
SoundCueAttackHit.Hint: Played on every attack-card result where the roll hits and is not a critical.

SoundCueAttackMiss.Name: Cue — attack misses
SoundCueAttackMiss.Hint: Played on every miss that is not a natural 1 fumble.

SoundCueAttackCrit.Name: Cue — critical hit
SoundCueAttackCrit.Hint: Played on a natural 20 attack result.

SoundCueAttackFumble.Name: Cue — critical miss
SoundCueAttackFumble.Hint: Played on a natural 1 attack result.

SoundCueDamageApplied.Name: Cue — damage applied
SoundCueDamageApplied.Hint: Played when non-zero damage lands on a target after the apply flow.

SoundCueSaveSuccess.Name: Cue — save succeeded
SoundCueSaveSuccess.Hint: Played when any save roll succeeds.

SoundCueSaveFail.Name: Cue — save failed
SoundCueSaveFail.Hint: Played when any save roll fails.

SoundCueConditionApplied.Name: Cue — condition applied
SoundCueConditionApplied.Hint: Played when a timed condition is applied to an actor (paralysis, poison, stun, blind, etc.).

SoundCueCinematicIntro.Name: Cue — cinematic intro
SoundCueCinematicIntro.Hint: Played when a Cinematic Roll Request banner opens on every client.

SoundCueCinematicSuccess.Name: Cue — cinematic success
SoundCueCinematicSuccess.Hint: Played at the outro phase when the aggregate cinematic result is a success.

SoundCueCinematicFailure.Name: Cue — cinematic failure
SoundCueCinematicFailure.Hint: Played at the outro phase when the aggregate cinematic result is a failure or mixed.

BloodiedThreshold.Name: Bloodied threshold (HP fraction)
BloodiedThreshold.Hint: Auto-apply the Bloodied status when current HP / max HP drops to or below this value. Default 0.5 (50%).

AutoRollInitiative.Name: Auto-roll initiative for new combatants
AutoRollInitiative.Hint: When a combatant is added to an active combat tracker without a rolled initiative, the GM client rolls it automatically.

CombatRoundSummary.Name: Post combat round summary card
CombatRoundSummary.Hint: On every round advance, whisper a chat card to the GM with initiative order, current HP, and fatigue level for each combatant.

TokenFatigueOverlay.Name: Show fatigue level on tokens
TokenFatigueOverlay.Hint: Render a small "F-N" badge in the top-right corner of any token whose actor has fatigue.round > 0.

Sheet.IncapacitatedTooltip: Cannot act while stunned, paralyzed, sleeping, or unconscious.
Sheet.ReadyPowers: Currently active or ready to fire.

Compose.GMOnly: Only the GM can request a cinematic roll.

RollRequest.Title: Request Roll
RollRequest.NoTargets: Select at least one actor for the request.

TraitsDamage.Hint: Hold Ctrl/Cmd to select multiple. Stacking order: immunity > vulnerability > resistance.

Effects.Title: Active Effects
Effects.Hint: Passive bonuses + temporary states applied to this character. Transferred effects from mutations and equipped items show their source; direct effects can be edited in place.
Effects.ItemHint: ActiveEffects attached to this item. With Transfers ON, each effect auto-applies to the actor when the item is on their sheet (and auto-removes on delete).
Effects.EmptyItem: This item has no ActiveEffects. Click "New Effect" to add one.

SkillSheet.Title: Skills & Proficiencies
SkillSheet.Hint: Each PC may mark up to 3 skills as proficient. Proficient rolls add a flat +2. Override a skill's ability if your character's mutation or build justifies a remap.
SkillSheet.MaxProficientWarn: Max of 3 proficient skills. Uncheck another first.

Weapon.AmmoTypesHint: Leave empty for weapons that never consume ammo. Select multiple types for weapons that accept alternates (e.g. the Needler fires poison OR paralysis darts).

Ammo.AutoDestroyHint: When this stack reaches zero rounds, the gear item is automatically removed. Disable to keep an empty quiver/clip in inventory for restocking.

Artifact.Title: Artifact
Artifact.AnalysisRequired: Unidentified Ancient item. Analyze to determine its function.
Artifact.ClearMalfunctionDone: Malfunction cleared — the artifact can be used again.
Artifact.ClearMalfunctionGmOnly: Only the GM can clear an artifact malfunction.
Artifact.AnalyzeNeedsActor: Open this artifact from a character's inventory to analyze it.

Analyze.Label: Analyze
Analyze.Hint: Open the artifact-analysis session to learn what this device is and how to operate it.
Analyze.Unidentified: Function unknown — analyze to learn what this artifact does.
Analyze.OperationUnknown: Operation unknown — analyze to learn how to use it.

Session.Title: Artifact Identification
Session.InterruptConfirm: Interrupt the current uninterrupted attempt and reset the visible path to Start?
Session.RevealFunctionConfirm: Reveal this artifact's function immediately and mark its operation as understood without analysis?
Session.RevealFunctionGmOnly: Only the GM can reveal an artifact's function.
Session.ResetConfirm: Clear this shared artifact session for everyone?
Session.WaitingHint: This client is waiting for the current artifact session state from the GM.
Session.NoRollsYet: No public rolls have been made yet.

Hint.Roll: Advance the analysis by one roll. The operator (or GM, if forcing) draws d10 + intelligence + speed modifiers on the current chart node.
Hint.Interrupt: Abandon the current uninterrupted attempt and reset the visible path to Start. Prior time and rolls are preserved in the history.
Hint.TryItOut: Test the artifact's operation once it's been understood. Triggers the function / malfunction check.
Hint.Close: Close this window. The session continues running in the background and remains accessible from the inventory.
Hint.Helpers: Additional actors assisting the analysis. Each helper grants extra rolls per hour.
Hint.HelpersMinus: Remove one helper from the pool.
Hint.HelpersPlus: Add one helper to the pool.
Hint.Reassign: Transfer operator status to another actor. Only the operator can roll the flowchart.
Hint.RevealFunction: Mark this artifact's operation as understood without further analysis. Flags it known and reveals its name publicly.
Hint.Reveal: Reveal the hidden function / malfunction outcome to everyone. Only fires after the outcome has been rolled.
Hint.Reset: Clear this session entirely and start over. Use to recover from stuck state or replay for testing.
Hint.Debug: Toggle display of internal node identifiers on the flowchart, useful for rule adjudication and reporting.

Session.OperationUnknown: This artifact's operation is not yet understood. Use the shared identification flowchart first.
Session.SuccessPublic: The artifact's function has been worked out.
Session.InstantSuccess: Molecular Understanding reveals the artifact instantly.
Session.InterruptedPublic: The attempt is interrupted; the visible trail resets to Start.

Public.Burst: A sharp crack and burst of energy lash out!
Public.PshReliable: PSH reliability: the artifact responds without hesitation, performing flawlessly.

Artifact.AnalysisSuccess: The artifact's purpose and basic operation are understood.
Artifact.ShortCircuit: The artifact shorts out during use.

PowerCell.AtomicNotRechargeable: Atomic cells are not rechargeable — swap fuel cylinders instead.
PowerCell.DisuseDrain: Chemical and solar cells lose their charge after 1d6 years of disuse.
PowerCell.ChargerIncompatible: This cell cannot be recharged by the Energy Cell Charger.

Consumption.Label: Consumption
Consumption.PerUnitHint: Percent of one cell drained per unit. Fractional values are allowed for sub-percent rates (e.g., 3.333 for a Needler's 30-dart budget).

Travel.Label: Travel
Travel.Hint: Travel for N hours. Rolls a wandering-encounter check per leg, advances world time, and consumes 1 ration per PC per 24 hours.
Travel.Prompt: How far does the party travel before the next stop?
Travel.BadInputs: Pick a terrain and duration before traveling.
Travel.CombatActive: Travel paused — finish the active combat first.
Travel.StoppedAt: Stopped at leg {n} due to encounter.

Encounter.NoScopeActors: No actors matched that encounter scope.
Encounter.MoraleContinue: Holds morale and continues the fight.
Encounter.RouteTimeNote: If an encounter occurs during route movement, the referee should still determine the exact time and location.

MutationMethod.Choose: Choose mutations, defects assigned by the referee rules

Rest.Label: Rest
Rest.Hint: Rest for 24 hours. Heals per the natural healing rate.

ShortRest.Label: Short Rest
ShortRest.Hint: Take a 1-hour rest. Spend up to 1/3 of your Hit Dice (each = 1d6 healing).
ShortRest.Prompt: Hit Dice to spend (0–{cap}; you have {available} available, level {level}):
ShortRest.NoHitDice: No Hit Dice remaining for a Short Rest.

LongRest.Label: Long Rest
LongRest.Hint: Take a 6-hour rest. Restores all HP and Hit Dice (unless poisoned or radiation-sick).
LongRest.Prompt: Take a 6-hour long rest? HP fully restores unless you are poisoned or suffering radiation sickness; Hit Dice refresh either way.

AwardXp.Label: Award XP
AwardXp.Hint: Award experience points to this character. Levels up automatically when thresholds are crossed.

ApplyBonus.Label: Apply Bonus
ApplyBonus.Hint: Spend the next pending attribute bonus (from level-up).

Error.ChargenAlreadyRolled: This character has already been rolled.
